reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify California counties with area median income of at least $142,800 in 2022 and at least $147,900 in 2023.
2. From those counties, keep the ones with qualified WIC families' average benefits redeemed of at least $570 in 2018 and at least $700 in 2020.
3. From the counties that remain, keep those with a 2015 perioperative hemorrhage or hematoma observed rate of at least 19.
4. From the counties that remain, keep those with at least 3 billion square meters of area.
5. Determine the county seat of the remaining county.
original_final_question: Identify a specific California county based on a set of health and housing statistics. The county had an area median income of at least $142,800 in 2022 and $147,900 in 2023. It also had qualified WIC families' average benefits redeemed of $570 or more in 2018 and $700 or more in 2020, a 2015 perioperative hemorrhage/hematoma observed rate of 19 or higher, and at least 3 billion square meters. Once you identify the county that matches all these figures, what is its county seat? Only report the county seat. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
